title: End-to-end transactional user-registration flow with three atomic jobs
prompt: Write `MyApp.Accounts.register_user/1` that transactionally creates a user and enqueues three related jobs, all in ONE Ecto.Multi with full idempotency and error handling. Requirements: (1) insert the user via a changeset, (2) enqueue `MyApp.Workers.WelcomeEmailWorker` with `unique: [period: 86_400, keys: [:user_id], states: [:available, :scheduled, :executing, :retryable, :completed, :cancelled, :discarded]]` (full safe state list), (3) enqueue `MyApp.Workers.OnboardingSeedWorker` with `schedule_in: 300` (5 minute delay), (4) enqueue `MyApp.Workers.CrmSyncWorker` with `priority: 9` (lowest priority). Each `Oban.insert/3` call must be a function that reads `user` from Multi context and returns a Worker.new/2 call with string-keyed args. Run with `Repo.transaction/1`. Return `{:ok, user}` or `{:error, failed_step, failed_value, _}`. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Every aspect of the binary family must be present and correct: Multi + unique (with full states) + priority inversion + schedule_in + string keys. A single oversight drops the score.
research_citation: Oban module HexDocs: 'Ecto.Multi.new() |> Oban.insert(...) |> MyApp.Repo.transaction()'; Elixir Forum thread 69648 on partial state lists.
